rows.follow_up_details: Assigned to Isaac Badillo on 5/26/26, Due 5/29/26 Is there a standard to which the shop needs to be sending out accumulators to the field with regards to fitting set up. - by Timothy Dibben on 5/26/26 Assigned to Jaelen Maslowski on 5/25/26 Relief aide needs to be male fitting - by Bryce Christoffersen on 5/25/26
rows.author: Bryce Christoffersen
rows.event_description: Deployment Zone Accumulator -010 has same fittings for pressure and relief sides
rows.root_cause_analysis: Field changed fittings to be same styie,
rows.immediate_action: Change fittings to be one maie and one female as standard.
rows.permanent_action: Discuss with Field Operations what is the best set up going forward
rows.action_effective: Monitor to see if a resolution is decided upon.

rows.follow_up_details: Assigned to Jaelen Maslowski on 5/26/26, Due 5/28/26 Work with both crews to understand that valves must be backed off 1/4 turn after full open or full close - by Timothy Dibben on 5/26/26 Assigned to Jaelen Maslowski on 5/25/26 Is this how we are transporting valves ? New process? - by Bryce Christoffersen on 5/25/26
rows.author: Bryce Christoffersen
rows.event_description: Deployment Audit Flowback HWO on cross All 6 trees cranked full open for deployment, NO 1/4 turn off. Had to hand off of 2 to get them to move.
rows.action_effective: Monitor that all personnel know how to properly set valves
